title: Extend hero image to top of page and add scroll-hiding 'Delicious Mexican Food' header text

requirements.summary: Extend the hero section image to fill the full viewport from y=0, removing any top offset or gap caused by the sticky header
acceptanceCriteria: The hero/banner image starts at the very top edge of the browser window with no gap or offset.
No white or colored space appears between the top of the viewport and the hero image.
The image extends behind the header on load.

file_operations.description: Remove top padding/margin from the hero container and adjust positioning to ensure the background image starts at y=0. Update the container styling to use absolute positioning or negative margin to extend behind the header. Ensure the hero section height accounts for content visibility while the image bleeds to the viewport top.

requirements.summary: Update the Header component to display 'Delicious Mexican Food' as the tagline and implement scroll-hide behavior
acceptanceCriteria: The header displays the text 'Delicious Mexican Food' as the tagline while visible.
The header slides out of view (upward) when the user scrolls down.
The header reappears when the user scrolls back up.
All other header elements (logo, navigation, phone number) remain unchanged.
The scroll-hide behavior uses the existing useScrollDirection hook.

file_operations.description: Change the tagline text from 'Delicious Mexican food for every special occasion!' to 'Delicious Mexican Food'. Import and use the useScrollDirection hook to track scroll direction. Add conditional CSS transform (translateY) to hide the header when scrolling down and show it when scrolling up. Apply smooth transition for the hide/show animation. Keep all existing header elements (logo, nav links, phone number, hamburger menu) unchanged.
